title: New Air Force One Lacks Defensive Countermeasures of Previous Model, Officials Say
summary: Experts said the lack of such capabilities poses a potential risk when the president travels overseas. The White House defended the aircraft’s safety.

title: Mexican Man Fatally Shot by ICE Wasn’t the Intended Target
summary: Officials say agents believed a passenger resembled one suspect, but the encounter quickly escalated into a fatal shooting.

title: What We Know About the ICE Shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo
summary: Mr. Araujo was a father, a husband and a business owner who had moved to the United States 35 years ago from Mexico.

title: Bombing Iran, Then a Tentative Accord Didn’t Work. Does Trump Have a Plan C?
summary: The administration appears to be reverting to an all-stick, no-carrot approach. But it has yet to answer why it believes economic warfare and bombing will yield a different result this time.

title: The U.S. and Iran Are Fighting Again. Is the Cease-Fire Over?
summary: Eric Schmitt, a New York Times National Security correspondent, analyzes recent skirmishes between the U.S. and Iran and whether they threaten ongoing negotiations for a peace deal.

title: As U.S. Steps Up Attacks on Iran, Prospect of All-Out War Rises
summary: American officials said they were trying to safeguard maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran said it had responded by firing at U.S. bases in Qatar, Bahrain and Kuwait.

title: Kirk Killing Suspect Confessed and Voiced Regret, Former Partner Says
summary: Tyler Robinson came home on the day of Charlie Kirk’s assassination and anxiously paced the floor, Lance Twiggs told investigators in an interview that was played in court.

title: Volkswagen Is Cutting Production as Sales in China Plunge
summary: The German automaker has struggled to compete with fast-growing Chinese companies that offer more affordable and sophisticated electric vehicles.

title: China, Russia and Others Seek to Inflame Debate Over A.I. Data Centers
summary: State actors in China, Russia and Iran have sought to exploit the U.S. public debate over the effects of the technology.

title: A $3.2 Trillion Deal-Making Frenzy Is Spurred by the A.I. Economy
summary: This year’s boom includes the most spent on global deal-making in a six-month period in a decade. But questions persist about whether it can continue.

title: As Workers Shore Up Midtown Building, Developer Reassures Investors
summary: Crews installed “temporary shoring” on more floors in the building, and the developer of the project has reassured investors that the project will be completed on time.

title: Inspector of Buckling Midtown Building Cited for Missing Problems at Other NYC Sites
summary: A New York Times review of city records shows that the firm, Domani Inspection Services, was repeatedly accused of breaking New York City rules.

title: Graham Platner’s Exit Sets Off Scramble for New Democratic Senate Candidate in Maine
summary: The state Democratic Party has said it will pick a replacement through a nominating convention before a July 27 deadline. Candidates are already lining up.

title: Maine Democrats Hope to Replace Platner as Senate Nominee. Has That Ever Worked?
summary: Graham Platner is one of just a handful of candidates in recent decades who have withdrawn from a Senate race after securing a major party nomination.

title: 12 People Die in Southern Spain Wildfire
summary: The blaze in Andalusia prompted a major containment operation. Heat waves across Europe this summer have raised the risk of such fires, forecasters say.

title: This Summer, Midtown Manhattan Is Taking Center Stage
summary: Midtown, with its high-rise office buildings and tourist hotels, isn’t hip, but it has become the site of a series of dramatic spectacles this year.

title: Trump Administration Fires Members of Independent Election Group
summary: The firings and a resignation render the Election Assistance Commission useless. The moves come as President Trump seeks to impose control over how ballots will be counted in the midterms.
